actor_claims.quote_text: our board of directors established a special committee of the board

actor_relation_claims.quote_text: On March 28, 2013, sTec entered into an engagement letter with BofA Merrill Lynch to provide financial advisory services.
Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher LLP (“Gibson Dunn”), which had been engaged in October 2012 as the company’s outside corporate counsel
Latham & Watkins LLP (“Latham & Watkins”), litigation counsel to sTec and Mr. Manouch Moshayedi
our board of directors established a special committee of the board, initially composed of Dr. Daly, and Messrs. Colpitts and Witte
actor_relation_claims.role_detail: initially composed of Dr. Daly, and Messrs. Colpitts and Witte
actor_relation_claims.subject_label: Special Committee of the sTec board of directors

actor_relation_claims.object_label: Special Committee of the sTec board of directors
actor_relation_claims.quote_text: our board of directors added Mr. Bahri to the special committee

bid_claims.quote_text: Company D provided a verbal indication of its interest to pursue a transaction at a price greater than $5.60 per share
On May 3, 2013, WDC submitted a written indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price in the range of $6.60 – $7.10 per share in cash.
Also on May 10, 2013, Company D submitted a written non-binding indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price of $5.75 per share in cash, along with a draft exclusivity agreement.
On May 15, 2013, Company H submitted a written non-binding indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price in the range of $5.00 – $5.75 per share in cash.
On May 28, 2013, WDC submitted a written second-round indication of interest at a price per share of $9.15 in cash
On June 10, 2013, WDC submitted a revised written indication of interest to acquire the company at a price range of $6.60 to $7.10 per share in cash.
On June 14, 2013, WDC submitted a written indication of interest of $6.85 per share in cash and indicated it could be in a position to announce a transaction on June 24, 2013.

event_claims.description: The board determined that it would be beneficial to undertake a formal review of strategic alternatives, including exploring a potential sale of sTec.
event_claims.quote_text: our board of directors determined that it would be beneficial to undertake a formal review of strategic alternatives, including exploring a potential sale of sTec

event_claims.description: In mid-November 2012, the board authorized management to contact potential financial advisors regarding strategic alternatives, including a potential sale.
event_claims.quote_text: in mid-November, 2012, our board of directors authorized management to contact potential financial advisors to explore their interest in representing the company in its exploration of strategic alternatives, including a potential sale of the company

event_claims.description: An investment bank representing Company A contacted sTec management to request a preliminary meeting.
event_claims.quote_text: On November 14, 2012, an investment bank representing Company A, a participant in the storage industry, contacted our management to request a preliminary meeting between sTec and Company A.

event_claims.description: A scheduled meeting between Company A and sTec was subsequently cancelled due to concerns expressed by non-management board members.
event_claims.quote_text: A meeting was scheduled between Company A and sTec, however, it was subsequently cancelled because of concerns expressed by non-management board members

event_claims.actor_label: Special Committee of the sTec board of directors
event_claims.description: The board established a special committee to review sTec's strategic alternatives, including a possible sale.
event_claims.quote_text: During the meeting on February 13, our board of directors established a special committee of the board, initially composed of Dr. Daly, and Messrs. Colpitts and Witte, and subsequently expanded to four members on March 26, 2013.

event_claims.description: Company B's President stated that he intended to present sTec as a possible acquisition target to Company B's management.
event_claims.quote_text: On February 13, 2013, Mr. Manouch Moshayedi met with the President of Company B, a participant in the electronics industry.

event_claims.description: Company B determined that it was not interested in acquiring sTec.
event_claims.quote_text: Company B’s management had determined that it was not interested in acquiring sTec and believed that it could independently develop the technology it needed to compete in enterprise SSDs.

event_claims.description: Company C expressed potential interest in exploring an acquisition of particular assets of sTec's business.
event_claims.quote_text: On March 13, 2013, Mr. Mark Moshayedi and Mr. Manouch Moshayedi met with representatives of Company C, a participant in the semiconductor industry

event_claims.description: The special committee decided not to continue discussions with Company C because Company C was only interested in limited, select assets.
event_claims.quote_text: On April 15, 2013, Company C indicated that it was only interested in purchasing limited, select assets of the company, and as a result, the special committee decided not to continue discussions with Company C.

event_claims.description: Company D contacted sTec management to express interest in exploring a potential acquisition of sTec.
event_claims.quote_text: In mid-March, 2013, the head of corporate development for Company D, a participant in the storage industry, contacted our management to express the interest of Company D in exploring a potential acquisition of the company.

event_claims.description: The board approved retaining BofA Merrill Lynch and determined to approach strategic buyers and conduct a limited confirmatory exploration of financial buyers.
event_claims.quote_text: In light of these and other factors, our board of directors determined that it was in the best interests of the company and its shareholders to confidentially approach strategic buyers that the board believed would be most likely to have interest in a potential acquisition of the company to explore their interest, but to also further conduct a limited confirmatory exploration of interest of financial buyers.

event_claims.description: sTec entered into an engagement letter with BofA Merrill Lynch to provide financial advisory services.
event_claims.quote_text: On March 28, 2013, sTec entered into an engagement letter with BofA Merrill Lynch to provide financial advisory services.

event_claims.description: At the special committee's direction, BofA Merrill Lynch began contacting potential acquirers of sTec.
event_claims.quote_text: On April 1, 2013, as directed by the special committee, BofA Merrill Lynch began contacting potential acquirers of sTec.

event_claims.description: BofA Merrill Lynch held discussions with 18 prospective acquirers, including 17 technology companies and one financial sponsor.
event_claims.quote_text: In total, BofA Merrill Lynch had discussions with 18 prospective acquirers, 17 of which were technology companies and one of which was a financial sponsor.

event_claims.description: sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company E.
event_claims.quote_text: On April 4, 2013, sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company E

event_claims.description: sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company D.
event_claims.quote_text: on April 10, it entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company D

event_claims.description: sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company F.
event_claims.quote_text: on April 11, it entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company F

event_claims.description: sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company G.
event_claims.quote_text: on April 17, it entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company G

event_claims.description: sTec entered into an addendum to its existing non-disclosure agreement with WDC.
event_claims.quote_text: Also on April 17, 2013, sTec entered into an addendum to its existing non-disclosure agreement with WDC.

event_claims.description: sTec executed six non-disclosure agreements related to exploring a potential sale of the company.
event_claims.quote_text: In total sTec executed six non-disclosure agreements related to the exploration of a potential sale of the company.

event_claims.description: sTec held management presentations with representatives of WDC, Company D, Company G and Company E during the weeks of April 8 through April 22, 2013.
event_claims.quote_text: During the weeks of April 8 through April 22, 2013, sTec held management presentations with representatives of WDC, Company D, Company G and Company E.

event_claims.description: Company F declined to schedule a management presentation and had no further communications regarding a possible transaction.
event_claims.quote_text: on April 24, Company F declined the invitation to schedule a management presentation, indicating it was only interested in purchasing limited, select assets of sTec, and Company F had no further communications with BofA Merrill Lynch or sTec regarding a possible transaction.

event_claims.description: The special committee decided not to continue discussions with Company E because it was only interested in limited, select assets.
event_claims.quote_text: Shortly thereafter, Company E indicated it was also only interested in purchasing limited, select assets of sTec, and as a result the special committee decided not to continue discussions with Company E.

event_claims.description: Members of sTec management met with WDC representatives regarding a possible transaction.
event_claims.quote_text: On April 18, 2013, members of our management held a meeting with representatives of WDC, at which our management provided a presentation on sTec, and the attendees discussed matters related to a possible transaction between the two companies.

event_claims.description: BofA Merrill Lynch sent process letters to WDC and Company D requesting non-binding indications of interest by May 3, 2013.
event_claims.quote_text: On April 23, 2013, at the direction of the special committee, BofA Merrill Lynch sent process letters to WDC and Company D requesting non-binding indications of interest by May 3, 2013.

event_claims.description: Company D provided a verbal indication of interest above $5.60 per share.
event_claims.quote_text: On the call, Company D provided a verbal indication of its interest to pursue a transaction at a price greater than $5.60 per share and detailed their additional diligence requirements for submitting a written indication of interest.

event_claims.description: BofA Merrill Lynch sent a process letter to Company G requesting a non-binding indication of interest by May 3, 2013.
event_claims.quote_text: Also on April 26, 2013, at the direction of our chairman on behalf of the special committee, BofA Merrill Lynch sent a process letter to Company G requesting a non-binding indication of interest by May 3, 2013.

event_claims.description: Company H contacted BofA Merrill Lynch expressing interest in a potential transaction with sTec.
event_claims.quote_text: On May 1, 2013, Company H contacted representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch expressing an interest in a potential transaction with sTec.

event_claims.description: WDC submitted a written indication of interest to acquire sTec for $6.60 to $7.10 per share in cash.
event_claims.quote_text: On May 3, 2013, WDC submitted a written indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price in the range of $6.60 – $7.10 per share in cash.

event_claims.description: Company G indicated it would not continue in the process.
event_claims.quote_text: Also on May 3, 2013, Company G indicated it would not continue in the process.

event_claims.description: sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company H.
event_claims.quote_text: Also on May 8, 2013, sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company H.

event_claims.description: sTec granted WDC access to the virtual data room.
event_claims.quote_text: Also on May 10, 2013, sTec granted access to WDC to the virtual data room.

event_claims.description: Company D submitted a written non-binding indication of interest at $5.75 per share in cash.
event_claims.quote_text: Also on May 10, 2013, Company D submitted a written non-binding indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price of $5.75 per share in cash, along with a draft exclusivity agreement.

event_claims.description: sTec granted Company D access to the virtual data room.
event_claims.quote_text: On May 14, 2013, sTec granted access to Company D to the virtual data room.

event_claims.description: Company H submitted a written non-binding indication of interest at $5.00 to $5.75 per share in cash.
event_claims.quote_text: On May 15, 2013, Company H submitted a written non-binding indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price in the range of $5.00 – $5.75 per share in cash.

event_claims.description: BofA Merrill Lynch told Company H its submitted price range was not sufficient to move forward in the process.
event_claims.quote_text: representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch contacted Company H and indicated that the price range Company H had submitted was not sufficient to move them forward in the process.

event_claims.description: BofA Merrill Lynch sent final round process letters and a draft merger agreement to WDC and Company D, requesting a response by May 28, 2013.
event_claims.quote_text: After the meeting, at the direction of the board, BofA Merrill Lynch sent final round process letters and a draft merger agreement to WDC and Company D, requesting a response by May 28, 2013.

event_claims.description: Company H remained interested but was not able to increase its indicated value range.
event_claims.quote_text: On May 23, 2013, representatives of Company H contacted a representative of BofA Merrill Lynch and indicated that Company H remained interested in a potential acquisition of the company but that Company H was not able to increase its indicated value range.

event_claims.description: WDC submitted a written second-round indication of interest at $9.15 per share in cash with merger agreement markup and ancillary agreement drafts.
event_claims.quote_text: On May 28, 2013, WDC submitted a written second-round indication of interest at a price per share of $9.15 in cash, with a mark-up of the merger agreement and drafts of ancillary agreements

event_claims.description: Company D stated it needed approximately two more weeks for diligence and to submit a written indication and merger agreement markup.
event_claims.quote_text: Also on May 28, 2013, a representative of Company D contacted representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch and indicated that Company D continued to have an interest in a potential acquisition and understood they needed to move meaningfully on value; however, the representative stated that Company D needed approximately two weeks of additional time to continue its ongoing due diligence review and to submit a written indication of interest and mark-up of the merger agreement.

event_claims.description: WDC verbally indicated its $9.15 per share indication was its best possible price.
event_claims.quote_text: On May 30, 2013, representatives of WDC verbally indicated that WDC’s indication of interest to acquire the company at a price of $9.15 per share was WDC’s best possible price

event_claims.description: The board unanimously determined to move forward with WDC on its proposed terms.
event_claims.quote_text: Our board of directors unanimously determined to move forward with WDC on their proposed terms, including endeavoring to complete diligence and negotiation of the merger agreement and ancillary agreements to be in a position to announce a transaction on June 3, 2013.

event_claims.description: BofA Merrill Lynch informed WDC that sTec would move forward at $9.15 per share subject to satisfactory negotiations.
event_claims.quote_text: representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch informed WDC that sTec would move forward to effect a transaction at a purchase price of $9.15 per share, subject to satisfactory negotiations of the merger agreement and ancillary agreements.

event_claims.description: WDC communicated that it was reevaluating its interest and was not prepared to move forward at that time.
event_claims.quote_text: On May 31, 2013, a representative of WDC communicated to BofA Merrill Lynch that WDC was reevaluating its interest in acquiring sTec, and that WDC was not prepared to move forward with a transaction with sTec at that time.

event_claims.description: Company D indicated it would not be in a position to actively conduct diligence for more than two weeks and was disengaging from the process.
event_claims.quote_text: On June 5, 2013 representatives of Company D contacted representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch and indicated that Company D would not be in a position to actively conduct due diligence for more than two weeks and was disengaging from the process.

event_claims.description: WDC submitted a revised written indication of interest at $6.60 to $7.10 per share in cash.
event_claims.quote_text: On June 10, 2013, WDC submitted a revised written indication of interest to acquire the company at a price range of $6.60 to $7.10 per share in cash.

event_claims.description: WDC submitted a written indication of interest of $6.85 per share in cash and stated it could announce a transaction on June 24, 2013.
event_claims.quote_text: On June 14, 2013, WDC submitted a written indication of interest of $6.85 per share in cash and indicated it could be in a position to announce a transaction on June 24, 2013.

event_claims.actor_label: Special Committee of the sTec board of directors
event_claims.description: The special committee unanimously determined that moving forward with WDC on the June 14 terms was in the best interests of sTec and its shareholders.
event_claims.quote_text: the special committee unanimously determined that they believed that it was in the best interests of sTec and its shareholders to move forward with a transaction with WDC on the terms set forth in WDC’s indication of interest received on June 14, 2013

event_claims.description: The board unanimously determined to move forward with WDC on the June 14 proposal terms, subject to final documentation.
event_claims.quote_text: the board members present unanimously determined that they believed that it was in the best interests of sTec and its shareholders to move forward with a transaction with WDC on the terms set forth in WDC’s proposal received on June 14, 2013

event_claims.description: BofA Merrill Lynch informed WDC that the board authorized moving forward at $6.85 per share, subject to final terms.
event_claims.quote_text: representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch informed WDC that our board of directors had authorized moving forward with a transaction with WDC on the terms set forth in WDC’s proposal received June 14, 2013, at a price of $6.85 per share

event_claims.description: WDC conducted additional due diligence from June 16 to June 22, 2013.
event_claims.quote_text: From June 16 to June 22, 2013, WDC conducted additional due diligence, including confirmatory due diligence calls.

participation_count_claims.quote_text: In total, BofA Merrill Lynch had discussions with 18 prospective acquirers, 17 of which were technology companies and one of which was a financial sponsor.
one of which was a financial sponsor
Of these, three prospective acquirers (WDC, Company H, a participant in the storage industry, and Company D) submitted written indications of interest for a complete purchase of sTec
six potential strategic acquirers expressed an interest in purchasing limited, select assets of sTec
nine prospective acquirers, including the financial sponsor and Company A, indicated they were not interested in a potential transaction.
In total sTec executed six non-disclosure agreements related to the exploration of a potential sale of the company.
During the weeks of April 8 through April 22, 2013, sTec held management presentations with representatives of WDC, Company D, Company G and Company E.

During the weeks of April 22 and April 29, 2013, sTec provided follow-up due diligence materials to, and at the direction of the special committee, BofA Merrill Lynch held additional discussions with, WDC, Company D, and Company G.
BofA Merrill Lynch sent final round process letters and a draft merger agreement to WDC and Company D
